title: Prosciutto Wrapped Chicken with Sage & Quinoa
summary: Impressive dinners usually mean piles of washing up - not this one. Wrapped in a simple parchment parcel, chicken with salty prosciutto and nutty quinoa cooks itself in the oven.

method.description: Heat the oven to 200°C. Lay out a large rectangle of baking paper in front of you, portrait. Arrange the Tenderstem Broccoli in a single layer on the bottom half of the paper leaving a 10cm gap between the edge closest to you and the food, then scatter over the quinoa. Drizzle with the olive oil, season with salt and black pepper and mix everything together. Repeat with the remaining ingredients.If you’re scaling up for more people, just use a foil-lined baking tray and a tight foil lid instead. Cooking times might vary, so keep an eye on it.

Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper. Place the sage leaves on top of each chicken breast, then wrap with prosciutto to seal. Lay the wrapped chicken on top of the veg and quinoa. Fold the top half of the baking paper over the food then fold the outside edges over each other to seal (like a cornish pasty), creating a steam-tight parcel. Transfer to the oven and cook for 15-20 mins, depending on the thickness of your chicken.

If you’d like crispy prosciutto, then heat the grill to high. Unwrap the parcel, remove the chicken and transfer to a baking tray. Slide it under the grill for 2 mins, or until the prosciutto is golden and crisp. Cut the lemon into wedges.
Squeeze over the lemon juice before serving and dig in.

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
